# Section 5: KTU 2024 Scheme Examination Question Bank & Topic Recap

---

## Part A: Short-Answer Questions (3 Marks Each)

### Q1. `[KTU University Exam - Dec 2023]`
**Define the ROS Navigation Stack. List any four of its constituent packages.**

**Model Answer (3 Marks):**
- (1 Mark) **Definition:** The ROS Navigation Stack is a collection of metapackages in ROS that enables a robot to autonomously navigate from a start pose to a goal pose by integrating sensor data, odometry, and a known map.
- (1 Mark) **`move_base`** — Central action server that coordinates global and local planning.
- (1 Mark) **`costmap_2d`** — Multi-layered 2D grid representing obstacles and inflation zones.
- (Bonus) Additional valid packages: `amcl` (localization), `map_server` (map loading), `navfn` / `global_planner` (path search), `dwa_local_planner` (local control).

---

### Q2. `[KTU University Exam - May 2024]`
**What is the role of the Inflation Layer in the costmap, and what is the formula for the inflation radius?**

**Model Answer (3 Marks):**
- (1 Mark) **Role:** The Inflation Layer adds a *safety margin* around obstacles, creating a repulsive cost field so that the global and local planners prefer paths farther from walls.
- (1 Mark) **Formula:**
$$r_{inflation} = r_{robot} + r_{obstacle} + \epsilon$$
- (1 Mark) **Explanation:** $r_{robot}$ is the robot's circumscribed radius, $r_{obstacle}$ is the obstacle inflation, and $\epsilon$ is a small safety buffer (typically 0.1–0.3 m). The default `cost_scaling_factor` is **10**, and the cost decays linearly from 253 at the obstacle edge to 0 at $r_{inflation}$.

---

## Part B: Full-Descriptive Questions (14 Marks — KTU ESE Internal Choice)

---

### Question A (14 Marks): `[KTU University Exam - Dec 2023]`
**(a) Explain the architecture of the `move_base` node. Describe the role of the global costmap, local costmap, global planner, and local planner with their default update frequencies. (7 Marks)**

**(b) Describe the Dynamic Window Approach (DWA) algorithm used by the local planner. Derive the trajectory update equations and the scoring function. (7 Marks)**

#### Model Solution

**(a) `move_base` Architecture (7 Marks):**

- (1 Mark) `move_base` is implemented as a **SimpleActionServer** over `move_base_msgs/MoveBaseAction`. It accepts `PoseStamped` goals in the `map` frame.
- (1 Mark) It maintains **two costmaps**:
  - **Global Costmap** — large area (~ 50 m × 50 m), updated at **1 Hz**, used by the global planner.
  - **Local Costmap** — small rolling window (~ 5 m × 5 m), updated at **5 Hz**, used by the local planner.
- (1 Mark) **Global Planner** (`navfn` / `global_planner`): Computes a long-horizon path using **A*** or **Dijkstra** on the global costmap. Output topic: `/global_plan` (`nav_msgs/Path`).
- (1 Mark) **Local Planner** (`dwa_local_planner`): Computes short-horizon velocity commands that track the global path while avoiding dynamic obstacles. Output topic: `/cmd_vel` (`geometry_msgs/Twist`).
- (1 Mark) **Recovery Behaviors:** Triggered when the robot gets stuck. Sequence (default): **clear_costmap → rotate → clear_costmap → wait → clear_costmap → rotate**. If still stuck, `move_base` returns **ABORTED**.
- (1 Mark) **Required I/O Topics:**
  - **Input:** `/tf`, `/odom`, `/scan` (or `/cloud`), `/map` (via `map_server`).
  - **Output:** `/cmd_vel`, `/move_base/status`, `/move_base/feedback`, `/move_base/result`.
- (1 Mark) **Coordinate Frames:** All goals must be in the `map` frame. The robot's pose is broadcast on `/amcl_pose` (if AMCL is used).

**(b) DWA Algorithm and Equations (7 Marks):**

- (1 Mark) **Concept:** DWA samples the **velocity space** $(v, \omega)$ that is reachable within one control cycle, given the current velocity and acceleration limits. It forward-simulates a trajectory for each sampled velocity and scores it.
- (2 Marks) **Dynamic Window Constraint:** The reachable set is:
$$
V_d = \bigl\{(v, \omega) \mid v \in [v_c - \dot{v}_{max} \Delta t,\ v_c + \dot{v}_{max} \Delta t],\ \omega \in [\omega_c - \dot{\omega}_{max} \Delta t,\ \omega_c + \dot{\omega}_{max} \Delta t]\bigr\}
$$
This is intersected with the global bounds $[v_{min}, v_{max}]$ and $[\omega_{min}, \omega_{max}]$.
- (2 Marks) **Trajectory Update (Unicycle Kinematics):**
$$
\begin{aligned}
x_{k+1} &= x_k + v \cos(\theta_k) \cdot \Delta t \\
y_{k+1} &= y_k + v \sin(\theta_k) \cdot \Delta t \\
\theta_{k+1} &= \theta_k + \omega \cdot \Delta t
\end{aligned}
$$
- (1 Mark) **Scoring Function:**
$$
G(v, \omega) = \sigma\bigl(\alpha \cdot \text{heading}(v, \omega) + \beta \cdot \text{dist}(v, \omega) + \gamma \cdot \text{velocity}(v, \omega)\bigr)
$$
where `heading` = alignment to path, `dist` = clearance to nearest obstacle, `velocity` = forward progress.
- (1 Mark) **Selection:** The velocity with the maximum $G(v, \omega)$ is published on `/cmd_vel` at **20 Hz**.

> [!WARNING]
> **KTU Examiner's Valuation Warning (Common Pitfalls):**
> 1. Students often **omit the normalization function** $\sigma(\cdot)$ — typically `sigma = 1.0` and is **mandatory** to balance the three terms of different scales.
> 2. Do NOT confuse the **dynamic window** (acceleration-limited) with the **velocity bounds** (hardware-limited). They are intersected.
> 3. The simulation time $T_{sim}$ is **NOT** infinite; it is typically **1.5–2.0 s**.
> 4. Students forget that DWA also has a **trajectory rollout visualization** in RViz via `/local_plan`.

---

### Question B (14 Marks): `[KTU University Exam - May 2024]`
**(a) Explain AMCL (Adaptive Monte Carlo Localization). Derive the particle update and weight computation equations. (7 Marks)**

**(b) Describe the ROS costmap_2d package. List and explain the different layers in the costmap stack. Show the cost equation for the Inflation Layer. (7 Marks)**

#### Model Solution

**(a) AMCL and Particle Filter (7 Marks):**

- (1 Mark) **Definition:** AMCL is a probabilistic localization algorithm that estimates a robot's pose $(x, y, \theta)$ in a known 2D map by maintaining a set of weighted particles. Each particle is a pose hypothesis with an associated weight (likelihood).
- (1 Mark) **Motion Update (Prediction):** For each particle $i$, given the odometry control $u_t$:
$$
x_t^{[i]} \sim p(x_t \mid u_t, x_{t-1}^{[i]})
$$
The sample is drawn from the odometry motion model (sampled Gaussian noise on $\Delta x, \Delta y, \Delta\theta$).
- (1 Mark) **Measurement Update (Correction):** The weight of each particle is updated based on how well the observed laser scan matches the map at the hypothesized pose:
$$
w_t^{[i]} = w_{t-1}^{[i]} \cdot p(z_t \mid x_t^{[i]}, m)
$$
where $p(z_t \mid x_t^{[i]}, m)$ is computed via **beam-range likelihood** or **likelihood field** model.
- (1 Mark) **Resampling:** Particles with low weights are eliminated; high-weight particles are duplicated. SUS (Stochastic Universal Sampling) is used for low-variance resampling.
- (1 Mark) **KLD-Sampling (Adaptive Particle Count):**
$$
N_{max} = \frac{1}{2\epsilon} \chi^2_{k-1, 1-\delta}
$$
- (1 Mark) **Pose Estimate:** The final pose is the **weighted mean** of all particles (or the highest-weight cluster).
- (1 Mark) **Topics:** AMCL subscribes to `/scan`, `/tf`, `/initialpose` (for global relocalization); publishes `/amcl_pose`, `/particlecloud`, `/tf` (`map → odom`).

**(b) Costmap 2D Package (7 Marks):**

- (1 Mark) **Definition:** `costmap_2d` is a 2D grid-based representation of the environment where each cell stores an integer cost in $[0, 255]$ indicating traversability.
- (1 Mark) **Cell Value Legend:**
  - **0:** FREE_SPACE
  - **1–252:** Increasing cost (INSCRIBED_INFLATED_OBSTACLE)
  - **253:** NO_INFORMATION
  - **254:** LETHAL_OBSTACLE
  - **255:** UNUSED
- (1 Mark) **Layer 1 — Static Map Layer:** Loads the pre-built occupancy grid from `map_server` (`.pgm` + `.yaml`).
- (1 Mark) **Layer 2 — Obstacle Layer:** Processes 2D `LaserScan` data; marks hits as LETHAL and the line-of-sight as FREE.
- (1 Mark) **Layer 3 — Voxel Layer:** 3D obstacle tracking using `PointCloud2`; can detect overhanging obstacles.
- (1 Mark) **Layer 4 — Inflation Layer:** Adds safety margin around obstacles. Cost equation:
$$
C(d) = \text{cost\_scaling\_factor} \cdot (r_{inflation} - d)
$$
- (1 Mark) **Layer 5 — Range Sensor Layer (optional):** Integrates ultrasonic / IR sensors.

> [!WARNING]
> **KTU Examiner's Pitfall Callout:**
> 1. **DO NOT** confuse the **global costmap** (used for path planning) with the **local costmap** (used for obstacle avoidance). They have **different parameters, sizes, and update rates**.
> 2. The **Inflation Layer** is the **most-misconfigured** layer. Setting `inflation_radius` too large makes the robot refuse to enter doorways; too small makes it scrape walls.
> 3. The costmap is published as `nav_msgs/OccupancyGrid` for the global map and as a `nav_msgs/GridCells` marker for the inflation visualization in RViz.
> 4. AMCL's KLD-sampling means the **particle count is dynamic** — don't hard-code it in your answers.

---

## Topic Recap & Important Things to Remember

> [!IMPORTANT]
> **Rapid Revision Checklist (High-Density Summary):**

- **Navigation Stack = `move_base` + `costmap_2d` + global planner + local planner + AMCL + `map_server`.**
- **Required Hardware Drivers:** `/odom`, `/scan` (or `/cloud`), `/tf`, `/cmd_vel`.
- **Mandatory TF Tree:** `map → odom → base_link → laser_frame`. Any break = navigation fails.
- **Costmap Cell Values:** 0 (free) → 254 (lethal) → 255 (unused). 253 = unknown.
- **Inflation Radius:** $r_{inflation} = r_{robot} + r_{obstacle} + \epsilon$. Default scaling factor = 10.
- **Global Planner Algorithms:** Dijkstra (navfn) and A* (global_planner). Cost: $f(n) = g(n) + h(n)$.
- **Octile Heuristic:** $h = \sqrt{2} \cdot \min(\Delta x, \Delta y) + \mid \Delta x - \Delta y \mid$.
- **DWA = Dynamic Window Approach.** Scores by: `heading` + `dist` + `velocity`, weighted by $\alpha, \beta, \gamma$.
- **DWA Trajectory:** Uses unicycle model: $x' = x + v \cos\theta \cdot \Delta t$.
- **AMCL = Particle Filter Localization.** Weights updated by $w_t = w_{t-1} \cdot p(z_t \mid x_t, m)$.
- **KLD-Sampling:** Adaptive particle count using chi-square bound.
- **Action Interface:** `move_base_msgs/MoveBaseAction` (Goal = PoseStamped, Result = success/fail).
- **Recovery Behaviors Sequence (default):** Clear → Rotate → Clear → Wait → Clear → Rotate. Failure = `ABORTED`.
- **Update Frequencies:** Global costmap @ 1 Hz, Local costmap @ 5 Hz, Controller @ 20 Hz.
- **Default Topics in / out:** `/scan`, `/odom`, `/map`, `/tf` (in); `/cmd_vel`, `/global_plan`, `/local_plan`, `/amcl_pose` (out).
- **Costmap Layers (in stacking order):** Static → Obstacle → Voxel → Inflation → Range.
- **The "Navigating in Code" exam focus areas:** TF tree integrity, costmap tuning, planner algorithm choice, parameter file structure (`*.yaml`).
- **Common KTU Viva Questions:**
  - *Why two costmaps?* (Different scales and update rates.)
  - *Why two planners?* (Global = strategic, local = tactical with dynamic obstacles.)
  - *What is `cost_scaling_factor`?* (Multiplier for inflation decay; default 10.)

<!-- SECTION_5_END -->
